what: Repeated-run confirmation for the exact-list-ledger edge: the single make-ledger headline upgraded to 5 runs per cell on a fixed seeded corpus, plus a strong-tier Claude cross-check and the keep-sensitivity of the cost margin.
harness: engine/ledger_seq.py (--chain --full, 5 runs/cell, graded by exact-match against a code-computed ground truth)
corpus: 30 incident reports, about 20,000 tokens each, 10 URGENT ids, identical every run (seeded)
note: Claude vs OpenAI only: the two arms with server-side context management at this scale. Gemini full-context (about 600k tokens carried) is slow and rate-limits on a 5-run repeat, so its single headline run stands without a repeat.

configs.read: both reliably exact; Claude about 65% cheaper at equal correctness
both reliably exact; Claude about 8% cheaper (more context retained per turn shrinks the margin)
Sonnet also reliably exact, so the correctness is not a cheap-tier fluke

conclusion: The single-run win holds across repeats: Claude returns the exact list 5/5 in every config and on both a cheap and a strong tier. The cost margin is config-sensitive (about 65% cheaper at keep=1, about 8% at keep=3), so the edge is reliable correctness plus a cost advantage whose size depends on the context-editing keep setting.
